title: Unforgettably Garlicky Crisp Irish Lamb Tagine
description: A garlicky and crisp Irish dish featuring lamb, perfect for a date night, with a unique touch of plain chocolate, all made in a ceramic tagine and gluten-free-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the lamb pieces with minced garlic, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Heat the olive oil in the ceramic tagine over medium-high heat on the stovetop.
Sear the lamb pieces until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove them from the tagine and set aside.
Add the pitted dates to the tagine and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the broken chocolate pieces to the tagine and stir until they are melted and the mixture is smooth.
Return the lamb pieces to the tagine, cover it with a lid, and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce is thickened.
Remove the tagine from the oven and sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top.
Let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
